locations_updated.name: Interior of the Old Man's Saklya
locations_updated.update: Detailed interior confirmed: clay-plastered, whitewashed walls; weapons hung on nails between two large glittering basins; cushions arranged along front wall; earthen floor; thin creaking outer door leads to inner rooms. Visual palette: bright white walls, polished metal basins catching light, dark earth floor, muted textile cushions. This is the primary scene location for Chunk 4 — Hadji Murad's clandestine meeting with the old man and Eldar.

timeline_events.event: Old man sends child to mosque to fetch his son; Hadji Murad enters the saklya through creaking outer door.
timeline_events.time: Night (arrival under cover of darkness, consistent with preceding chunks)
timeline_events.location: Interior of the Old Man's Saklya

timeline_events.event: Woman arranges cushions for the guest. Hadji Murad and old man exchange traditional greetings ('May thy coming bring happiness' / 'May thy sons live').

Hadji Murad removes burka, rifle, sword — old man hangs weapons on wall beside household weapons. Hadji Murad adjusts pistol, sits on cushions. Both men pray together (palms upward, stroking faces/beards).
Old man cryptically reports no good news; speaks in parable ('hares and eagles') about Russian oppression; mentions Russians burned hay in Mitchit Aoul.
Eldar (Hadji Murad's murid) enters the saklya, removes weapons, hangs them beside his leader's. Old man seats him on felt beside Hadji Murad.
